pattern: Two sweeps, left-to-right then right-to-left, independently satisfy each neighbour constraint, and taking the max per child meets both at once for the minimum total.
hints: Every child needs at least one candy, and a child rated higher than a neighbour must get strictly more than that neighbour.
Handle the two directions separately: one pass enforces 'more than the left neighbour when rated higher', another enforces the same against the right neighbour.

Initialise all to 1; left-to-right, if ratings[i] > ratings[i-1] set candy[i] = candy[i-1] + 1; right-to-left, if ratings[i] > ratings[i+1] set candy[i] = max(candy[i], candy[i+1] + 1); sum the array.

edge_inputs.description: a valley between two higher ratings
equal neighbours need no extra over each other
long descent after a peak forces a tall right sweep
plateau then descent, equals do not chain
all equal ratings each get one

stress.description: random ratings over a small range to create many peaks and valleys
stress.note: small value range maximises ties and adjacent peaks/valleys
